category_title: Elementary student with ASD and limited verbal communication who protests via screaming and falling to the floor
minimal_context: Sylvia is sitting one-on-one with an educator for a 30-minute work session to complete academic assignments.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Sylvia, it is time for our work session. Here is a new assignment for you to look at.

STUDENT: Wonder Pets to the rescue.

TUTOR: Just try your best on this new worksheet.

STUDENT: (Looks away from the task and attempts to leave the designated classroom area.)

TUTOR: Please stay in your seat and focus on the paper.

STUDENT: (Screams, cries, and falls to the floor on her knees, kicking to protest the unfamiliar activity.)

options.A: Operate a level method during the sessions, determining the student's behavioral levels and implementing the rights they imply.
options.B: Use instruments as figurative media during the sessions, incorporating items like images, posters, and toys such as balls or blocks.
options.C: Invest time in focusing on environmental factors, such as clarifying expectations, establishing routines, and ensuring a structured program.
options.D: Complete consulting notes on a daily basis to share session information and strategies with other educators working with the student.
